main/about.html.title: About
main/about.html.description: Welcome, dear Guinea Pigs! This is my Obsidian Vault where I keep track of my various projects at the insistence of my peers.Here you will find information, internal documentation, logs, and general ramblings about my various experiments and projects as I create them.How often this will update, I don't actually know! I am not usually one to document my progress or projects like this, I typically prefer to keep mental track of what I do, but obviously that has its flaws.Either way, I hope you all enjoy.

projects/hardware/digiuma/digiuma-the-fr.title: DigiUma - The Framework for Umamusume Prosthetics.
projects/hardware/digiuma/digiuma-the-fr.description: A project aiming to develop upon the concept of biologically controlled animal prosthetics, focusing on Umamusume/Horse Girls.Over my time in the Umamusume fandom, I have seen something unique in myself and many other people of the community. There exists a rampant desire to become one of these fictional horse girls, sometimes on the same level as a transgender person may desire to become their desired gender, so much so that it spawned its own subgenre of the community. As inexplicable as it is, it is hard to deny that it exists. After spending some time in those specific spaces, I was inspired to create this project after being reminded of the Necomimi Brainwave Cat Ears. This project aims to develop on the concept.The scope of DigiUma is relatively small. I intend to create a set of fully articulated Ears and an articulated Tail, driven by a bespoke Behaviour Engine running on an ESP32-S3. The entire project aims to be as cheap as possible, provided you have the tools at hand already.Eventually, I would love to continue past that point into exoskeleton-based enhancements, but that will have to wait.The progress of this project will be marked in log form, and I will be creating them as I develop upon the project. You can find them starting here: TBW.
projects/hardware/digiuma/digiuma-the-fr.sourcePath: Projects/Hardware/DigiUma/DigiUma - The Framework for Umamusume Prosthetics..md

projects/hardware/digiuma/parts-list.htm.title: Parts List
projects/hardware/digiuma/parts-list.htm.description:
This is an internal document that I use to keep track of the parts I use during the development of DigiUma, mostly because a lot of my tools have been lost to time and I need to keep track of purchasing them all again. This should not be trusted as a guide.Copy-paste Icons: ✓ ✗
ESP32-S3 Dev Board ✗
x4 MG90s Micro Servos (specifically the MG version to prevent gear stripping) ✗
a lot of hot glue and patience ✗
80W 110V Soldering Iron ✗
A beautiful set of Helping Hands with a magnifying glass ✗
63-37 High Purity In Lead Rosin Core Solder ✗
Flux. Lots of flux. ✗

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
